continuum_comparison.note: Continuum S3 has constant positive curvature; discrete graph curvature approaches this only at large n_max when cycles form.

reasons: 1. Ollivier-Ricci curvature is scale-invariant under uniform edge weight rescaling. The topology alone determines curvature, NOT kappa.
2. At n_max=3 (tree): all OR curvatures are non-positive (mean=0.0000). Trees cannot have positive discrete Ricci curvature.
3. At n_max=4 (5 independent cycles): OR curvature range [0.0000, 0.0000], std=0.0000. NOT constant, NOT all positive.
4. kappa = -1/16 enters the Hamiltonian H = kappa*(D-A) + W as a uniform scaling of the kinetic (off-diagonal) terms. It sets the ENERGY SCALE, not the graph geometry.
5. The S³ constant curvature (Ric=2, K=1) is a continuum property. The GeoVac graph at any finite n_max is too coarse to have constant positive discrete Ricci curvature.
